summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the facil.io HTTP parser ignores Transfer-Encoding: chunked when Content-Length appears first in the request headers. A request with Content-Length: 5 before Transfer-Encoding: chunked causes the parser to read 5 bytes as the body, treating remaining data as a second HTTP request, demonstrating request smuggling.
expected_security_impact: The server should process two separate HTTP requests: the original POST /first (with 5-byte body per Content-Length) and a smuggled GET /smuggled (from data after the CL body). The GET /smuggled request appearing in server logs confirms that Transfer-Encoding was ignored, violating RFC 7230 Section 3.3.3.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched the facil.io HTTP server, sent the crafted request with Content-Length before Transfer-Encoding, and the server log shows both POST /first and GET /smuggled being processed as separate requests. This confirms the parser used Content-Length for body parsing and ignored Transfer-Encoding: chunked.
verification_method: The verify.sh script starts the server with request logging, sends the crafted smuggling payload via Python, and checks the server log for EVOHUNT_SMUGGLED_DETECTED. The presence of GET /smuggled in the log confirms that facil.io processed a second request from the remaining data after the Content-Length body, proving Transfer-Encoding was ignored.
evidence_produced_by_run: Server log shows 'POST /first' as the first request with a 5-byte body matching Content-Length.
Server log shows 'GET /smuggled' as a second request, parsed from data after the CL body.
The EVOHUNT_SMUGGLED_DETECTED marker appeared in the server log, confirming the smuggled request was processed.
This behavior violates RFC 7230 Section 3.3.3 which requires Transfer-Encoding to override Content-Length.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable tag v0.7.4, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
